17688135.summary: Zegona Communications' Vodafone Spain subsidiary delivered Q1 FY27 revenue growth of 2% to €916m, with EBITDA up 5% to €465m and EBITDAaL up 3% to €324m. Broadband lines grew 22k to 2,592k and mobile lines increased 345k to 12,864k. Net debt fell 11% to €3.2bn with leverage improving to 2.33x. A €3.7bn refinancing completed 14 July 2026 will deliver approximately €60m in annual interest savings, reducing annual debt costs from €294m (two years ago) to €170m going forward.
17688135.brief: Zegona reports Q1 FY27 results showing third consecutive quarter of growth with improved leverage and successful refinancing.
17688135.take: Consistent operational growth, deleveraging progress, and successful refinancing demonstrate execution of Zegona's strategy; materially lower future interest costs strengthen cash generation outlook.

17688155.summary: Defence contractor Cohort delivered record revenue of £306.4m (2025: £270.0m) and adjusted operating profit of £36.3m (2025: £27.5m), with adjusted EPS up 14% to 61.9p. Order intake reached £314.2m with record closing order book of £618.8m extending deliveries to 2037. Net margin improved to 11.8% from 10.2%. Communications and Intelligence division drove growth, particularly from EM Solutions acquisition. The company tripled its bank facility to £175m and proposes 10% dividend increase. Management targets double-digit earnings growth for next three years with 88% of FY26/27 revenue already underwritten by order book.
17688155.brief: Cohort PLC reports record FY26 results with 13% revenue growth to £306.4m and 32% adjusted operating profit increase to £36.3m, ahead of expectations.
17688155.take: Strong operational delivery ahead of consensus with commanding order book visibility, growing NATO European exposure, and improving margins likely appeal to growth investors; diversification away from UK MoD (now 40%) reduces concentration risk, but geopolitical dependency warrants monitoring.

17688137.summary: Getech Group has secured a multi-year agreement with a European-headquartered oil and gas super-major for access to its Globe subsurface intelligence platform, valued at $660k in annual recurring revenue across three years. This contract brings five of the world's six recognised oil and gas super-majors onto Globe's client base. CEO Chris Jepps highlighted strengthened new business pipeline and growing geopolitical importance of energy security driving exploration demand.
17688137.brief: GETECH wins multi-year Globe contract with European oil & gas super-major worth $660k over three years.
17688137.take: Positive indicator of growing ARR momentum and competitive moat with super-majors; reflects market tailwinds from energy security concerns and reserve replacement pressure.

17688138.summary: Dr. Martens held its AGM on 15 July 2026 and confirmed FY27 guidance remains unchanged. Trading since the financial year start aligns with expectations. The company is progressing four strategic objectives: driving full-price revenue in UK and DACH markets, launching an innovative sandals range, opening new retail concepts globally, and unlocking operating model benefits. USA (largest market) continues growing with strong wholesale performance; Japan and South Korea performing well; European markets tracking expectations amid challenging consumer conditions.
17688138.brief: Dr. Martens holds AGM; maintains FY27 guidance with on-track strategic execution across all four pillars.
17688138.take: Broadly reassuring interim update with no surprises, but lacks specific metrics or upgrades; market likely to view this as steady-state positive with the USA momentum offset by European caution.

17688139.summary: Gamma Communications purchased 16,908 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 via Investec at prices ranging from 906p to 925p, with weighted average price of 917.2p. Since programme launch on 13 January 2026, company has repurchased 2.68m shares and intends to cancel them. Post-settlement, 91.08m shares remain in issue with 89.57m voting shares (excluding 1.51m treasury holdings). Company is in offer period as announced 7 April 2026.
17688139.brief: Gamma Communications repurchased 16,908 shares on 14 July at 906-925p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
17688139.take: Routine buyback execution during offer period; share cancellation reduces share count but is non-event absent offer context. Market impact likely neutral unless offer details drive sentiment.

17688140.summary: AdvancedAdvT Limited (AIM: ADVT) purchased 15,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at 157.60 pence per share, held in treasury. Cumulative buyback total now stands at 5,218,100 shares under the programme announced 4 March 2026. Following purchase, total voting rights equal 131,907,706 shares, with 5,218,100 held in treasury from 137,125,806 issued shares.
17688140.brief: AdvancedAdvT Limited purchases 15,000 own shares at 157.60p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
17688140.take: Routine share buyback continuation suggests management confidence in value at current prices; modest weekly purchase indicates measured capital allocation rather than aggressive action.

17688141.summary: Costain has been awarded a three-year extension to its Managed Service Provider contract with United Utilities, extending the partnership from 2019 through the remainder of AMP8 and with an option to extend to end of AMP9 in 2035. The team of 120+ professionals has delivered over 900 capital maintenance projects and 9,000 responsive maintenance activities to date. United Utilities is investing £13.5bn in water and wastewater infrastructure during AMP8.
17688141.brief: Costain secures three-year United Utilities contract extension, extending partnership to ten years through 2035.
17688141.take: Positive signal of contract stability and customer confidence; demonstrates Costain's execution track record in regulated utilities sector, supporting revenue visibility through 2035.

17688142.summary: UK construction group expects full-year revenue growth of circa 3% and adjusted profit before tax at the top end of analyst forecasts (£51.4m-£53.4m range). Average month-end cash increased 21% to £216.2m, with year-end cash of £258.8m and no bank debt. The group completed a £10m share buyback programme and deployed £38.4m in capital allocations. Order book stands at £4.3bn with 90% of new financial year revenue secured across public sector frameworks in water, transport, energy, education and defence.
17688142.brief: Galliford Try reports sixth consecutive year of growth with adjusted PBT at top end of £51.4m-£53.4m forecast range.
17688142.take: Strong operational momentum, robust balance sheet and substantial forward order book suggest durable earnings trajectory; likely read as confidence-building ahead of September results, though modest 3% revenue growth and margin progression toward 4% target may temper enthusiasm.

17688143.summary: GlobalData PLC purchased 97,946 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 through Investec Bank plc at prices ranging from 73.10p to 76.70p, with a weighted average price of 74.4810p. The shares will be cancelled. Following this purchase, total voting rights reduce to 745,998,027 shares, with no shares held in treasury. This transaction forms part of the company's Share Buyback Programme that commenced 23 June 2026.
17688143.brief: GlobalData repurchased 97,946 shares at 74.48p average as part of ongoing buyback programme commenced 23 June 2026.
17688143.take: Routine share buyback execution within disclosed programme; modest weekly volume suggests measured capital allocation but immaterial to overall valuation. Non-event for market.

17688144.summary: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited repurchased 74,735 USD-denominated ordinary shares at GBP 4.510 per share on 14 July 2026, to be held as treasury shares. Total treasury shares now stand at 6,524,914, with 120,554,609 shares in issue. The total voting rights in the company are 120,554,609 shares.
17688144.brief: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund repurchased 74,735 shares at GBP 4.51 per share on 14 July 2026.
17688144.take: Routine share buyback disclosure; treasury share repurchases are standard capital management practices with no material implications for shareholders unless done at materially depressed valuations.

17688145.summary: Babcock International Group purchased 161,880 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at prices ranging from 993.4p to 1015.0p, with a volume-weighted average price of 1004.31p. Since 1 July 2026, the company has repurchased 816,537 shares costing £8.24m. Following this transaction, Babcock holds 13.39m treasury shares with 492.20m shares in issue. All purchases executed via J.P. Morgan Securities plc on London Stock Exchange.
17688145.brief: Babcock repurchased 161,880 shares on 14 July 2026 at average price of 1004.31p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
17688145.take: Routine capital management; buyback demonstrates management confidence in valuation but signals no new strategic developments. Treasury share accumulation typically neutral for equity holders unless coupled with earnings growth or special returns.

17688146.summary: The company purchased 433 ordinary shares of 10 pence each on the London Stock Exchange at a weighted average price of 809.20 pence per share via J.P. Morgan Securities plc. The shares will be cancelled. Post-transaction, the company holds 3,318,207 treasury shares and has 11,492,905 ordinary shares in issue (excluding treasury shares).
17688146.brief: Barings Emerging EMEA Opportunities repurchased 433 shares at 809.20p per share for cancellation on 14 July 2026.
17688146.take: Routine share buyback for cancellation; modestly accretive to EPS but small scale suggests minimal material impact on shareholder value.

17688148.summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC purchased 250,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 through Investec Bank plc at a weighted average price of 134.80p. The shares will be held as treasury stock. Following this transaction, HICL holds 166,095,988 treasury shares, with 1,865,392,073 shares remaining in issue (excluding treasury). The total voting rights denominator for disclosure purposes is 1,865,392,073 shares.
17688148.brief: HICL Infrastructure repurchases 250,000 shares at 134.80p, adding to treasury holdings.
17688148.take: Routine share buyback within established programme; modest scale and single-day execution suggest orderly capital management rather than market signal. No material read-through for retail investors.

17688149.summary: Edinburgh Investment Trust purchased 50,000 ordinary shares (25p each) on the LSE on 14 July 2026 via Investec Bank at an average price of 812.00p per share. The shares will be held in treasury. Following the transaction, the company holds 69,564,709 treasury shares against total issued capital of 195,666,734 shares, leaving 126,102,025 shares with voting rights.
17688149.brief: Edinburgh Investment Trust repurchased 50,000 shares at 812p each on 14 July, holding them in treasury.
17688149.take: Routine share buyback reducing share count; typical capital management activity for investment trusts. Market likely treats as neutral or modest positive given disciplined capital deployment.

17688150.summary: The Renewables Infrastructure Group Limited purchased 270,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 73.20p through Investec Bank plc under its share buyback programme announced August 2024. Following this transaction, TRIG holds 152,637,374 treasury shares. Total voting rights excluding treasury shares stand at 2,333,325,512.
17688150.brief: TRIG buyback: 270,000 shares at 73.20p on 14 July as part of August 2024 programme.
17688150.take: Routine execution of announced buyback programme; share price stable at single execution price suggests orderly market conditions with no material news.

17688151.summary: AEP Plantations purchased 58,479 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at prices ranging from 164.00p to 169.00p, with a volume-weighted average price of 167.77p, via Cavendish Capital Markets. The shares are held in treasury. Since the buyback programme's announcement on 6 July, AEP has acquired 304,757 shares in aggregate. Following this purchase, the company has 399,762,720 shares in issue, with 16,473,997 treasury shares and 383,288,723 voting shares outstanding.
17688151.brief: AEP Plantations buys back 58,479 shares at 164-169p as part of announced programme.
17688151.take: Routine capital management execution within announced parameters; buyback at mid-range prices suggests orderly execution rather than opportunistic timing—no obvious market signal either way.

17688152.summary: International Workplace Group PLC repurchased 155,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 across multiple venues (LSE, CBOE, Turquoise, Aquis) at a volume-weighted average price of £1.8895 per share, ranging from £1.8750 to £1.9160. This brings total share buybacks under the programme announced 31 December 2025 to 39,545,069 shares. Following this purchase, IWG has 954,743,496 shares in issue (excluding treasury shares). The shares will be cancelled or held in treasury.
17688152.brief: IWG purchased 155,000 shares on 14 July 2026 at average price of £1.8895, bringing total buyback to 39.5m shares since December 2025.
17688152.take: Routine capital management disclosure. Share buybacks at stable prices around £1.89 suggest orderly execution and shareholder returns, but provide no insight into operational momentum or strategic direction. Standard compliance filing.

17688154.summary: Greencoat Renewables PLC executed a share buyback on 14 July 2026, purchasing 271,961 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin via RBC Europe Limited at prices ranging from €0.7530 to €0.7630 per share, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7590. The shares will be cancelled. Following settlement, the company holds 200,000 treasury shares and has 1,083,665,503 shares in issue, excluding treasury shares. This transaction forms part of the company's share buyback programme announced on 5 March 2026.
17688154.brief: Greencoat Renewables bought back 271,961 shares at €0.7530-€0.7630 on 14 July as part of its March 2026 programme.
17688154.take: Routine capital management execution; buyback programmes are standard practice for listed renewable energy companies seeking to optimise shareholder returns and manage share count, typically viewed as neutral to slightly supportive.

17688136.summary: The property company's current financial year (ending 25 March 2027) has begun in line with expectations. Wynnstay completed a new letting in Uckfield at increased rent, renewed three leases in Aylesford and Heathfield, and agreed terms on four additional renewals in Norwich and Stevenage, all at or above March valuation estimates. Two vacant Liphook units are now under offer, bringing the portfolio to near full occupancy. First quarter rent collection stands at 99%.
17688136.brief: Wynnstay Properties reports strong trading start to FY2027 with successful lettings and lease renewals across portfolio.
17688136.take: Positive operational update demonstrating effective tenant retention, rental growth, and strong lease collection rates ahead of AGM, suggesting portfolio performing ahead of valuation assumptions.

17688163.summary: Glanbia PLC bought back 22,114 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at €23.70 per share on Euronext Dublin via J&E Davy, with shares to be cancelled. This purchase forms part of the company's €50 million share buyback programme announced 25 February 2026 and running through 30 September 2026. Following settlement and cancellation, Glanbia will have 238,953,852 ordinary shares in issue.
17688163.brief: Glanbia repurchases 22,114 shares at €23.70 as part of €50m buyback programme through September 2026.
17688163.take: Routine share buyback update within announced programme; signals capital allocation to shareholders but is a non-event absent material programme changes or market concerns.

17688116.summary: Air China's June 2026 combined passenger capacity (ASK) declined 6.1% year-on-year while passenger traffic (RPK) fell 2.9%. Domestic routes saw steeper declines (capacity -7.7%, traffic -6.7%), but international routes showed traffic growth of 7.4% despite capacity declining 1.6%. Passenger load factor improved to 83.9% (+2.8 percentage points), with international routes up 7.0 points. Cargo capacity fell 6.3% but load factor increased 2.3 points. Fleet expanded to 972 aircraft, with two new routes launched in June.
17688116.brief: Air China reports June 2026 operating data showing mixed results with capacity and passenger declines offset by load factor improvements.
17688116.take: Mixed signals: demand weakness in domestic markets and overall capacity cuts are concerning, but strong international growth, improving load factors, and higher margins suggest operational efficiency gains and strategic repositioning toward international routes.

17688266.summary: Neuberger Private Equity Partners Limited repurchased 30,000 Class A shares on 14 July 2026 at £14.75 per share under shareholder authority granted 11 June 2026, via Jefferies International. All purchased shares will be cancelled, reducing outstanding Class A shares to 40,859,149. The company retains 3,150,408 Class A shares in treasury.
17688266.brief: NBPE bought back 30,000 Class A shares at £14.75 on 14 July 2026; shares to be cancelled.
17688266.take: Routine capital management activity; share cancellations typically neutral-to-positive as they reduce share count and can support per-share metrics, though scale here is modest relative to issued capital.

17688382.summary: Dominic Claridge, with 35+ years mining operations experience across Australia, Europe, Asia and Africa, appointed CEO effective immediately. Services provided via MET Mineral Resources Limited at £192,000pa, combined with Head of Exploration Sam Bolton. Mark Thompson steps down from Executive to Non-Executive Chairman (salary reduced from £60,000 to £30,000pa). Two related-party transactions approved by independent directors: MMR arrangement and Pure Reports Limited accounting services (£35,000pa, saving £20,000 annually).
17688382.brief: Tamar Minerals appoints mining veteran Dominic Claridge as CEO; Mark Thompson moves to Non-Executive Chairman.
17688382.take: Appointment of experienced mining executive strengthens operational credibility for tin/copper explorer; related-party structures disclosed transparently suggest governance discipline, though cost arrangements warrant scrutiny for value-for-money.

17688381.summary: Sunbelt Rentals Holdings Inc. (NYSE: SUNB; LSE: SUNB) completed the sale of $450m of 4.950% Senior Notes due 2030 and $750m of 5.650% Senior Notes due 2036, totalling $1.2bn in aggregate principal. The issuance was previously announced and filed via Form 8-K with the SEC on 14 July 2026.
17688381.brief: Sunbelt Rentals completes $1.2bn senior notes issuance across two tranches.
17688381.take: Routine debt capital raise with no material refinancing pressure signals; market likely neutral unless rates context suggests refinancing concerns or growth-funded capex.

17688263.summary: Fee-earning AUM reached $88bn (up 3% quarter-on-quarter, 10% year-on-year constant currency) with $2.4bn net additions. Fundraising totalled $4.1bn in Q1, led by Structured Capital & Secondaries raising $2.2bn including Europe IX's €2.1bn close (standing at €11bn, targeting €12bn by Q2, 50% larger than prior vintage). Deployment reached $3bn in Q1 (LTM $14.3bn). Dry powder stands at $36bn ($18bn not-yet-earning). Fitch reaffirmed BBB+ rating; BofA appointed joint broker; final FY26 dividend 59.3p payable 31 July.
17688263.brief: ICG reports Q1 FY27 AUM of $126bn, fee-earning AUM up 10% YoY to $88bn; Europe IX flagship fund on track to close at €12bn.
17688263.take: Strong quarter showing organic growth, successful flagship fundraising significantly oversubscribed, and solid deployment activity signals healthy market conditions and investor demand for ICG's strategies.

17688264.summary: Pershing Square Holdings purchased 37,377 public shares through Jefferies on 14 July 2026 at an average price of 51.75 USD. The buyback comprised 25,990 shares on LSE (PSH ticker) at 3,863 pence and 11,387 shares (PSHD ticker) at 51.75 USD. The NAV per share was 77.24 USD as of 7 July 2026. Post-buyback, PSH has 173.9 million shares outstanding, with 37.1 million held in treasury.
17688264.brief: PSH repurchases 37,377 shares at average price of 51.75 USD on 14 July 2026.
17688264.take: Routine share buyback at modest discount to NAV; typical capital allocation activity for closed-ended funds managing share price discount. No material read-through for investors.

17688416.summary: FTSE 250 closed-ended investment company TwentyFour Income Fund (TFIF) delivered NAV total return of 6.85% for year ended 31 March 2026 versus 13.61% prior year. Full-year dividend of 10.81p per share significantly exceeded 8p annual target despite lower interest rate environment (2025: 11.07p). Net assets rose to £926.53m from £843.79m; company raised £178.2m new funds since April 2025 including successful October 2025 realisation opportunity. Market cap reached £996m as of 10 July 2026. Portfolio includes ABS, RMBS and CLOs; trading at average 1.41% premium. No portfolio defaults since 2013 inception.
17688416.brief: TwentyFour Income Fund reports 6.85% NAV return and 10.81p dividend, beating 8p target despite lower rates.
17688416.take: Modest NAV return offset by continued dividend outperformance and strong capital raising, suggesting consistent income delivery amid market volatility. Expansion into US/Australia and shift to floating-rate positioning supports resilience in 'higher for longer' rates environment.

17688360.summary: As at 15 July 2026, BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc had 90,472,743 ordinary shares of £0.001 each in issued capital, excluding 27,456,195 treasury shares. This figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules 5.6.1. The announcement is a routine regulatory notification required under Article 15 of the Transparency Directive.
17688360.brief: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust updates total voting rights denominator to 90.5m shares.
17688360.take: Routine regulatory disclosure updating the share count denominator for major shareholding notification calculations; no material implications for investors.

17688365.summary: BlackRock American Income Trust plc disclosed its issued share capital consisted of 61,060,138 ordinary shares carrying one vote per share as at 15 July 2026, with 34,301,167 shares held in treasury. The company notified shareholders that this figure should be used as the denominator for calculating notification thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1. This is a routine regulatory filing required under Article 15 of the Transparency Directive.
17688365.brief: BlackRock American Income Trust updates voting rights denominator to 61.06m ordinary shares as at 15 July 2026.
17688365.take: Routine disclosure of voting rights denominator for shareholder notification threshold calculations; no material implications for investors or trading outlook.

17688370.summary: Barbara Clark, Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer at Sunbelt Rentals Holdings, Inc., had an administrative correction to a prior notification. On 19 June 2026, 2,819 shares of common stock were withheld at $86.06 per share to satisfy U.S. income tax obligations from vesting performance stock units under the 2026 Omnibus Equity Incentive Plan. The amendment corrects an error whereby 784 fewer shares than intended were originally withheld for taxes.
17688370.brief: Sunbelt Rentals amends director shareholding disclosure; Barbara Clark withholding correction on tax obligations.
17688370.take: Routine disclosure of a technical correction to director tax withholding; no material change to holdings or strategic significance; administrative housekeeping without market implications.

17688372.summary: Portfolio Manager Charlotte Cuthbertson will deliver a live presentation on the Annual Results for MIGO Opportunities Trust plc on Monday, 27 July 2026 at 10:00 BST via Investor Meet Company. The presentation is open to existing and potential shareholders, with pre-event questions accepted until 26 July at 9:00 BST. Investors can register free on the platform or will be automatically invited if already following the trust.
17688372.brief: MIGO Opportunities Trust to host investor presentation on 27 July via Investor Meet Company platform.
17688372.take: Routine disclosure providing access to management commentary on annual results; facilitates investor engagement but signals no material news or corporate action.

17688375.summary: Tamar Minerals plc (AQSE: TMR), the tin and copper exploration company, has restructured its board effective 15 July 2026. Dominic Claridge has been appointed Chief Executive. Mark Thompson transitions to Non-Executive Chairman, Brett Boynton joins as Non-Executive Director alongside existing Non-Executive Director Andrew Wilson. Sam Quinn has stepped down from the board immediately. Thompson thanked Quinn for his contributions and counsel to the board.
17688375.brief: Tamar Minerals restructures board: Dominic Claridge becomes CEO, Mark Thompson moves to Non-Executive Chairman, Sam Quinn exits.
17688375.take: Routine board reshuffle with new CEO appointment; lack of detail on Claridge's background or strategic direction limits investor assessment of significance or capabilities.

17688376.summary: BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust plc has notified the market of its issued capital as at 15 July 2026, comprising 100,226,997 ordinary shares of £0.01 each, excluding 35,359,197 shares held in treasury. The figure of 100,226,997 shares (excluding treasury holdings) should be used as the denominator for calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA rules.
17688376.brief: BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust updates voting rights denominator to 100.2m shares.
17688376.take: Routine regulatory disclosure establishing the voting rights denominator for shareholder notification purposes. No material implications for investors or trading activity.

17689467.summary: All nine resolutions at Primorus Investments plc's AGM were approved. Resolution 1-5 and 7-9 received overwhelming backing with 47+ million votes in favour against minimal opposition (51,251-99,691 votes against). Resolution 6 showed lower support with 7.3 million votes for, 51,251 against, and 40.2 million withheld, suggesting potential shareholder concerns on that specific matter. Voting details were substantially identical to the Notice of AGM published 19 June 2026.
17689467.brief: Primorus Investments PLC reports all AGM resolutions passed with strong shareholder support on 15 July 2026.
17689467.take: Routine regulatory disclosure showing standard AGM approval; Resolution 6's high abstention rate warrants attention but unlikely material market impact absent further context.

17689466.summary: Aircraft lessor Avation PLC repurchased 10,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at 137.5 pence per share, to be held in treasury. Following the transaction, 61,425,369 shares remain in issue with 1,580,068 treasury shares held, reducing voting rights to 59,845,301. The repurchase is conducted under the company's approved share buyback mandate authorising repurchase of up to 25% of issued capital, with authority valid until the next annual general meeting.
17689466.brief: Avation repurchases 10,000 shares at 137.5p per share under approved buyback programme.
17689466.take: Routine capital management activity; modest share count reduction signals management confidence but minimal market impact given small buyback scale and ongoing fleet leasing business operations.

17689458.summary: Hon Hai's subsidiary Hyield Venture Capital acquired 900,000 common shares of Churchill Capital Corp XI at USD 10 per share on 15 July 2026, totalling USD 9 million. The investment represents 0.3% shareholding in Churchill Capital Corp XI with no restrictions on rights. The transaction was approved by Hon Hai's board and constitutes 0.14% of total assets and 0.3% of shareholder equity, classified as a long-term investment under the partnership agreement.
17689458.brief: Hon Hai subsidiary Hyield invests $9m in Churchill Capital Corp XI common shares for long-term holding.
17689458.take: Minor portfolio diversification by Hon Hai subsidiary with immaterial impact to consolidated financials; routine corporate investment activity unlikely to move markets significantly.

17689456.summary: NCC Group and Siemens have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to strengthen cyber security across UK critical national infrastructure. The collaboration focuses on securing operational technology (OT) in industrial, energy, and defence sectors by combining Siemens' expertise in industrial automation and control systems with NCC Group's cyber security capabilities. The partnership targets the convergence of IT and OT environments where data systems connect with physical operations, aiming to support asset owners and supply chains with end-to-end cyber resilience solutions.
17689456.brief: NCC Group and Siemens sign MOU to collaborate on UK critical infrastructure cyber security, combining OT and IT expertise.
17689456.take: This MOU represents a commercial opportunity for NCC Group in the growing critical infrastructure cyber security market, though it is non-binding and lacks specific financial terms or revenue commitments, making it a modest positive signal rather than material news.

17689457.summary: Metro Bank Holdings PLC has disclosed that Dorita Gilinski, a non-executive director at the bank, has been appointed as a non-executive director of GeoPark Limited (NYSE: GPRK), effective 14 July 2026. The announcement was made in compliance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.9R(2), which requires listed companies to disclose director appointments at other organisations. This is a routine regulatory disclosure of an external directorship.
17689457.brief: Metro Bank director Dorita Gilinski appointed non-executive director of GeoPark Limited effective 14 July 2026.
17689457.take: Routine disclosure of director's external appointment; no material implications for Metro Bank's operations or strategy. Market likely to treat as non-event.

17689442.summary: Ryan Mangold, Executive Director and Chief Financial Officer of FirstGroup plc, purchased 83 ordinary shares of 5p each on 10 July 2026 at £1.8105 per share through the FirstGroup Share Incentive Plan, with a total value of £150.27. The purchase was made via Computershare Trustees Limited and notified on 15 July 2026 in compliance with Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014.
17689442.brief: FirstGroup CFO Ryan Mangold purchases 83 shares via company Share Incentive Plan at £1.81.
17689442.take: Routine director share purchase under employee plan; modest transaction size suggests standard PDMR disclosure rather than material confidence signal.

17689439.summary: Prudential PDMRs including CEO Anil Wadhwani, CFO Ben Bulmer, and other senior leaders acquired ordinary shares via the All Employee Share Purchase Plan on 10 July 2026. Total transactions: 272 shares purchased across eight executives at GBP 10.429195 per share. Acquisitions ranged from 36 to 40 shares per individual, representing routine insider share acquisitions at consistent pricing.
17689439.brief: Eight senior Prudential executives acquired shares through employee share purchase plan on 10 July at GBP 10.43 per share.
17689439.take: Routine disclosure of insider share purchases through employee scheme showing management alignment with shareholders. Non-event; no material significance for equity investors as these represent standard employee benefit plan participation.

17689431.summary: Dr. Martens held its AGM on 15 July 2026 with all 20 resolutions passing decisively. Ordinary resolutions (1-16) achieved 85-100% support, including approval of annual accounts, remuneration report, and final dividend. Director re-elections showed strong backing, with independent directors Robyn Perriss, Andrew Harrison, Ian Rogers, and Robert Hanson receiving 94.9-96.6% independent shareholder votes. Special resolutions authorising share allotment, pre-emption rights disapplication, share buybacks, and 14-day meeting notice all passed with 94.7-99.8% support. Voting participation reached 82.35% of issued capital.
17689431.brief: Dr. Martens AGM: all 20 resolutions passed with strong majorities on 15 July 2026.
17689431.take: Routine disclosure with overwhelming shareholder endorsement across all resolutions, including board re-elections and capital management authorities. No contentious votes or governance concerns. Non-event for markets.

17689429.summary: Hongkong Land Holdings completed a share repurchase of 510,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at prices ranging from US$7.07 to US$7.25 per share, with a weighted average purchase price of US$7.1187. The repurchased shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, issued share capital comprises 2,134,890,826 ordinary shares with one vote each. The company holds no treasury shares.
17689429.brief: Hongkong Land repurchased 510,000 shares on 14 July at average price of US$7.12 per share; shares to be cancelled.
17689429.take: Routine share buyback reducing share count; typical capital management action. No material value signal given modest buyback size relative to total issued capital.

17689426.summary: Costain Group purchased 504,928 ordinary shares between 8-14 July 2026 at prices ranging from 204p to 210p per share under its £20m buyback programme announced March 2026. Total shares bought to date: 5,064,190 for cancellation. Following settlement, issued shares will number 264,691,845. Panmure Liberum completed the first £10m tranche (excluding expenses); Investec Bank now commences the second £10m tranche from 15 July.
17689426.brief: Costain completes first £10m tranche of share buyback; 504,928 shares bought at 204-210p this week.
17689426.take: Routine capital management executing announced policy; demonstrates capital discipline and supports per-share metrics. No major signals either way—buybacks at modest single-digit premium to book suggest confidence but market context unchanged.

17689424.summary: Cohort PLC's final dividend for the year ended 30 April 2026 will be payable on 2 October 2026 to shareholders on the register at 21 August 2026, subject to AGM approval on 24 September 2026. The announcement notes record revenue and adjusted operating profit ahead of expectations. The defence technology group operates seven businesses across communications, intelligence, sensors and effectors segments, employing over 1,700 staff globally.
17689424.brief: Cohort PLC announces final dividend timetable for year ended 30 April 2026, citing record revenue and adjusted operating profit.
17689424.take: Positive signal: record profits and ahead-of-expectation performance support dividend continuation, though specific dividend amount and yield are not disclosed in this timetable announcement.

17689401.summary: NRIF, the debt issuance vehicle for Network Rail, reported profit before tax of £110k (2025: £110k). Finance costs were £1,497m (2025: £1,416m), offset by finance income. The company made a £701m loss on fair value of debt due to interest rate movements, partially offset by £16m gain on derivatives. Net borrowings increased to £27,504m from £27,324m. UK RPI index-linked debt represents 92% of gross debt. Directors confirmed going concern, noting planned absorption of Network Rail into Great British Railways under the Railways Bill; the financial indemnity from the UK Government remains intact.
17689401.brief: Network Rail Infrastructure Finance reports full-year results for year ended 31 March 2026; £110k profit before tax.
17689401.take: Non-event for retail investors. NRIF is a pass-through financing vehicle with all costs recovered from Network Rail via intercompany loan. The £701m fair value loss is non-cash and accounting-driven; economic substance unchanged. Government guarantee provides implicit backstop.

17689393.summary: Harwood Capital LLP and Oryx International Growth Fund Limited, controlled by Christopher Harwood and Bernard Mills, reduced their combined voting rights in Avingtrans PLC from 7.911% to 6.282% (2.32m shares) as of 13 July 2026. The notification was filed on 15 July 2026 following a disposal of voting rights that crossed the major holdings threshold downward.
17689393.brief: Harwood Capital's stake in Avingtrans falls below 7% threshold after shareholding reduction.
17689393.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a stake reduction below the 7% threshold. No immediate implications for retail investors unless signaling broader fund strategy shifts.

17689382.summary: UBS AG issued a post-stabilisation notice on 15 July 2026 confirming that no stabilisation measures were undertaken by the stabilising manager during the offering of €500 million fixed rate senior unsecured notes issued through ELM B.V. on behalf of Julius Baer Group Ltd. The notes were not offered in the United States and remain unregistered under US securities law.
17689382.brief: UBS confirms no stabilisation activity occurred during Julius Baer's €500m senior notes issuance.
17689382.take: Routine post-issuance disclosure confirming standard market practice; no stabilisation intervention suggests orderly order book at pricing, with minimal market impact implications.

17689384.summary: Norman Crighton, Non-Executive Director of RM Infrastructure Income PLC, tendered 11,945 ordinary shares during a recent tender offer on 1 May 2026 at £0.7477 per share, totalling £8,931.28. His holding reduced from 29,982 shares to 18,037 shares, representing 0.03% of issued share capital. The transaction was conducted on the London Stock Exchange.
17689384.brief: RM Infrastructure Income PLC director Norman Crighton tenders 11,945 shares in recent offer.
17689384.take: Routine director dealing disclosure via tender offer; modest share reduction by non-executive; no material significance for investors.

17689383.summary: STV Group plc will release its half-year results for the period ended 30 June 2026 on Tuesday, 8 September 2026. An analyst presentation will be hosted at 12:30pm via video conference by Chief Executive Rufus Radcliffe and Chief Financial & Operating Officer Lindsay Dixon. Interested parties should contact Angela Wilson at angela.wilson@stv.tv to attend.
17689383.brief: STV Group to publish H1 2026 results on 8 September with analyst presentation.
17689383.take: Routine disclosure of results publication date and presentation details; no material information released; market impact dependent on results content when published.

17689380.summary: Invesco Bond Income Plus Limited confirms all inside information held by directors has been disclosed to RNS, clearing the way for securities dealing. The company has entered a mandatory closed period under UK Market Abuse Regulation effective 15 July 2026, lasting until half-year results for the period ended 30 June 2026 are published. No new undisclosed inside information currently exists.
17689380.brief: Invesco Bond Income Plus enters mandatory closed period ahead of half-year results announcement.
17689380.take: Routine regulatory compliance disclosure signalling pre-results trading restrictions; standard practice for listed funds with no material implications.

17689379.summary: Amundi Physical Metals plc issued 35,000 ETC Securities on 15 July 2026 under Tranche 882 of its Amundi Physical Gold ETC programme. Metal entitlement per security: 0.03961782 fine troy ounces. Total series aggregate post-issuance: 66,983,037 units. Expense ratio: 0.12% per annum. Securities admitted to trading on LSE, Euronext Paris/Amsterdam, Deutsche Börse, Borsa Italiana, and Mexican Stock Exchange. Estimated net proceeds: USD 5.6m.
17689379.brief: Amundi Physical Metals plc issues 35,000 additional gold ETC securities (Tranche 882), bringing total series to ~67m units.
17689379.take: Routine capital raise for established precious metals ETC with wide multi-exchange listing. Standard issuance mechanics; no material news for sentiment direction. Non-event for most retail investors.

17689377.summary: Bank of America Corporation published a supplementary prospectus dated 15 July 2026, approved by the FCA, relating to its $85 billion Euro Medium-Term Note Program. The supplement incorporates by reference the issuer's Form 8-K filed 14 July 2026 containing earnings results for the three months ended 30 June 2026. The prospectus updates the base prospectus dated 13 May 2026 and excludes certain presentation materials and supplemental information from incorporation.
17689377.brief: Bank of America publishes supplementary prospectus incorporating Q2 2026 earnings disclosure for its $85bn Euro Medium-Term Note Program.
17689377.take: Routine regulatory disclosure updating debt prospectus with latest earnings; standard practice for ongoing note programs and immaterial for equities investors.

17689375.summary: Amundi Physical Metals plc (GLDA) issued 35,000 ETC securities on 15 July 2026 as Tranche 882 of its Amundi Physical Gold ETC programme. The securities carry a Metal Entitlement of 0.03961782 fine troy ounces and a Total Expense Ratio of 0.12% per annum. Total ETC securities outstanding post-issuance: 66,983,037. Authorised Participants include HSBC, Jane Street, Flow Traders, Optiver, BNP Paribas, and Virtu Financial. Securities are listed on Euronext Paris, Amsterdam, Deutsche Börse, Borsa Italiana, LSE, and Mexican Stock Exchange. Scheduled maturity: 23 May 2118.
17689375.brief: Amundi Physical Metals issues 35,000 gold-backed ETC securities (Tranche 882) with 0.12% annual fee.
17689375.take: Routine capital raising announcement for established gold ETC product. Tranche issuance is standard operational activity with no material disclosure implications for investors.

17689374.summary: On 14 July 2026, Lynden Jones, Chief Executive Officer of Touchstar plc (AIM: TST), purchased 50,000 ordinary shares in four tranches at prices between 80.4 and 81.6 pence per share, totalling £40,281. Following the purchase, Jones now holds 500,000 shares representing approximately 6.3% of the Company's issued voting share capital. Touchstar is a logistics software and operational infrastructure provider serving industrial sectors including transport, warehousing, and fuel distribution.
17689374.brief: Touchstar CEO Lynden Jones purchases 50,000 shares, increasing stake to 6.3% of issued voting capital.
17689374.take: CEO share purchase signals confidence in the business and aligns management interests with shareholders; modest positive signal but routine for executives to build stakes periodically.

17689373.summary: Standard Chartered PLC will hold a Board Committee meeting on 29 July 2026 to approve half-year results for the six months ended 30 June 2026 and consider an interim dividend. Subject to approval, the dividend record date is set for 7 August 2026, with different times for UK and Hong Kong registers (10.00pm London time and 9.00am Hong Kong time respectively). Share transfers must be lodged by 4.30pm Hong Kong time on 6 August 2026 to qualify for dividend entitlement.
17689373.brief: Standard Chartered sets 29 July board meeting to approve H1 2026 results and interim dividend payment.
17689373.take: Routine procedural announcement confirming interim dividend dividend timetable; no material substance regarding dividend amount, rate, or earnings guidance disclosed.

17689371.summary: Ronald Lansdell, subject to notification obligations, crossed a voting rights threshold on 13 July 2026, with his stake in Jersey Oil and Gas PLC reducing to 2.97% (970,601 voting rights). The holding falls below the 3% notifiable threshold, triggering this standard disclosure requirement under DTR5 rules.
17689371.brief: Ronald Lansdell's holding in Jersey Oil and Gas falls below notifiable threshold to 2.97%.
17689371.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major shareholder stake reduction; no material implications for equity valuation or company direction absent other context.

17689360.summary: Standard Chartered PLC has confirmed the appointment of Manus Costello as Group Chief Financial Officer and Executive Director with immediate effect following receipt of regulatory approvals. The appointment was initially announced on 17 May 2026. Costello joins the board of Standard Chartered PLC, which operates in 54 markets globally with a dual listing on London and Hong Kong exchanges.
17689360.brief: Standard Chartered appoints Manus Costello as Group Chief Financial Officer, effective immediately, following regulatory approval.
17689360.take: Routine executive appointment disclosure confirming previously-announced GCFO hire with regulatory clearance obtained; likely non-event for markets absent further context on Costello's background or strategy implications.

17689290.summary: Adam Daniels, Chief Executive Officer of Vistry Group PLC, purchased 38,372 ordinary shares of 50p each on 15 July 2026 at £2.606 per share via the London Stock Exchange, representing a total investment of £99,997.43. This is an initial notification of the transaction under PDMR shareholding disclosure requirements.
17689290.brief: Vistry CEO Adam Daniels purchases 38,372 shares at £2.61 for ~£100k on 15 July 2026.
17689290.take: CEO share purchase signals management confidence in the company's value at current levels; modest position size suggests cautious rather than aggressive conviction, likely viewed as mildly positive but not material.
